title: RustFS
description: High-performance, S3-compatible distributed object storage written in Rust, with a built-in web console; a drop-in MinIO/Ceph alternative.
installation: RustFS is itself the datastore: it persists all objects and metadata on disk under /data using erasure coding, so no database add-on is required. The S3-compatible API and web console are both served on container port 9000 (console reachable at /rustfs/console). Set RUSTFS_VOLUMES to the data path. IMPORTANT: rotate RUSTFS_ACCESS_KEY and RUSTFS_SECRET_KEY before exposing the endpoint - the template defaults (rustfsadmin / replace-with-openssl-rand-hex-32) are root credentials. After first boot, log in to the console with those credentials, then create scoped access keys and buckets for your apps. For multi-node erasure-set durability, run several replicas and point RUSTFS_VOLUMES at multiple mounts; this single-node template gives one volume suitable for dev/small workloads. No external S3, search engine, or SMTP service is needed.
